records.ety: [L. necessarius, from necesse unavoidable, necessary; of uncertain origin: cf. F. ncessaire.]

defs: Such as must be; impossible to be otherwise; not to be avoided; inevitable.
quotes: Death, a necessary end, Will come when it will come.

defs: Impossible to be otherwise, or to be dispensed with, without preventing the attainment of a desired result; indispensable; requisite; essential.
quotes: A certain kind of temper is necessary to the pleasure and quiet of our minds.

defs: Acting from necessity or compulsion; involuntary; -- opposed to free; as, whether man is a necessary or a free agent is a question much discussed.
A thing that is necessary or indispensable to some purpose; something that one can not do without; a requisite; an essential; -- used chiefly in the plural; as, the necessaries of life.
Such things, in respect to infants, lunatics, and married women, as are requisite for support suitable to station.
